body.content: Waves and ocean rendering in WebGL is quite popular (see this famous shadertoy example using procedural noise and this reference threejs example using FFTs). These examples are impressive but they rely entirely on fragment processing. The Gerstner approach documented in GPU Gems 1 is geometry-oriented; it is more suitable in a number of scenarios, as it puts less strain on the GPU and is more model-friendly (e.g. to synchronize ship position and pitch-roll-yaw).
